page: Amazon Web Services m8gd.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 192 dedicated physical cores based on the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture, operating at 2.7 GHz with no hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 768.0 GB of memory, 11400 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier for multi-core processing and memory bandwidth. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test yielded a score of 314716.23, and the passmark memory score reached 3307.03. Memory read-only bandwidth for large blocks was recorded at 895.14 GB/sec, while write bandwidth reached 425.78 GB/sec. The instance also demonstrated top-tier performance in database operations, achieving 364.75 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and a score of 46746.5 in PassMark database operations. Single-core performance, such as the stress-ng single-core score of 1643.39, falls within the average tier.

The ARM64 architecture and high resource density of the m8gd.48xlarge provide high qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded workloads and memory-intensive operations. However, single-threaded tasks and certain LLM inference workloads, such as prompt processing for small models, show lower relative performance. This instance is highly suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing workloads that benefit from high-capacity local NVMe storage and high memory bandwidth.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance utilizing 192 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 cores operating at 2.7 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 768.0 GB of RAM, 11400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 314716.23, and exceptional memory bandwidth, reaching 895.14 GB/sec for large block reads. The instance excels in database operations, achieving 364.75 million operations per second in Redis SET tests, and software compilation. While single-core performance and small-model LLM prompt processing are average to weak, the high core density and massive local storage offer excellent qualitative cost efficiency for parallelized workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, multi-threaded data processing, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.48xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 192 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 768 GB RAM, and 11,400 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.48xlarge is an ARM64 instance with 192 cores, 768 GB RAM, and 11,400 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for database workloads.
tagline: A high-density ARM64 instance featuring 192 dedicated cores, massive local NVMe storage, and top-tier multi-core and database performance.
bullet_points: Powered by 192 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 cores at 2.7 GHz.
Equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory and 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and high-speed memory bandwidth.
Achieves exceptional throughput in database operations and Redis SET tests.
Best suited for large-scale databases, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing.
